You are running as a single automated phase of the ADW pipeline.
The orchestrator performs ALL git and GitHub work for this run: do NOT run git or gh, do NOT create/switch/commit/push branches, and do NOT open, merge, or comment on pull requests. If the task section below tells you to do any of that, skip those steps.
You have no GitHub access in this phase; all issue context you need is provided inline.

---

Classify work item #1 into a single change type for branch naming and commit prefixing.

Work item context (title, body, labels):

1 GitHub issue #1: docs: declare Claude-only MVP scope + live-run playbook
Labels: issue_class:docs adw-live-batch

Part of the MVP live-run batch (`adw_sdlc/docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`). Intended `issue_class`: **docs**. Planned ADW run mode: **forced-fenced** (`ADW_PARITY_FORCE_FENCED_JSON=1`).

## Goal
Declare the MVP scope as **(A) Claude ships real issues reliably** and add a short live-run playbook, so `MVP-READINESS.md` §0 stops reading "unset".

## Scope
- In `adw_sdlc/MVP-READINESS.md` §0, record the decision: MVP = (A); demote (B) four-runner and (C) cutover to post-MVP.
- Add a brief "How to run a live `claude` issue" section that points at `docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md` and the `npm run verify` gate.
- Keep `(B)`/`(C)` gates listed but clearly marked post-MVP.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] §0 shows MVP = (A) explicitly (no "unset").
- [ ] A live-run playbook subsection links `docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`.
- [ ] `npm run verify` stays green (docs-only change).

## Notes
Docs-only; no code paths change. Good low-risk smoke test and the forced-fenced parity baseline.

## Instructions

- Choose exactly one `issue_class` from: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `test`, `refactor`.
  - `feat` — new user-visible feature or capability.
  - `fix` — bug fix or correctness change.
  - `docs` — documentation-only change.
  - `chore` — maintenance, deps, tooling with no user-visible behavior change.
  - `ci` — CI/build/workflow change.
  - `test` — test-only change.
  - `refactor` — internal restructuring with no behavior change.
- Prefer the narrowest truthful classification.
- If labels and body disagree, classify by the actual requested change.


---

## Required output

End your reply with EXACTLY one fenced ```json block matching this shape, and nothing after it:

```json
{"issue_class": "feat|fix|docs|chore|ci|test|refactor", "reason": "<one sentence>"}
```
